analysis.overall_assessment: Both detected tea leaves exhibit severe necrotic damage with extensive dark spots and discoloration, indicating critical health deterioration. No viable healthy tissue remains for premium processing.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Install anti-pollution windbreaks (e.g., bamboo screens) around tea plots to filter airborne SO₂
Apply calcium-based soil amendments to neutralize acidification from SO₂ byproducts
Implement real-time air quality monitoring with harvest suspension protocols during high-pollution periods

immediate_actions: Halt processing of affected batch immediately
Isolate leaves in sealed containers to prevent cross-contamination
Conduct lab testing for residual sulfur compounds
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Complete rejection of all leaves; no salvageable portions for commercial processing
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not applicable - leaves must be diverted to waste stream
quality_preservation: If any marginally affected leaves are found in future batches, process separately as low-grade 'kukicha' twig tea
Use controlled oxidation to mask minor defects in borderline cases

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: None - all leaf tissue shows advanced necrosis
alternative_uses: Soil amendment for non-edible crops after composting
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Layer with 3:1 ratio of carbon-rich materials (sawdust, straw), maintain 55-65°C for 14 days to neutralize pollutants
prevention_measures: Establish 50m buffer zone with SO₂-absorbing species (e.g., willow, poplar)
Switch to sulfur-free fertilizers and organic pest control methods
Implement predictive modeling for pollution events using weather data

cost_saving_opportunities: Convert waste stream to certified organic compost for sale to neighboring farms
Claim crop insurance for pollution-related losses with documented air quality reports
value_recovery_methods: Produce limited-edition 'remediation tea' from future healthy harvests with pollution-control storytelling
Partner with environmental agencies for pollution mitigation grants

priority_actions: Install air quality sensors with SMS alerts for SO₂ levels above 0.03 ppm
Conduct soil sulfur testing and initiate remediation within 2 weeks
Train harvesters to identify early-stage pollution symptoms using mobile app diagnostics
recommendations.timeline: Immediate (0-7 days) for containment, 30 days for soil remediation, 90 days for full mitigation system implementation
monitoring_points: New leaf emergence health (biweekly visual inspection)
Soil pH and sulfur content (monthly testing)
